title: Dal Makhni
description: Slow-simmered whole urad and rajma in a smoky tomato-cashew gravy—restaurant richness without butter or cream.
explanation: Dal makhni’s soul is time: whole black urad breaks down into a silky body while a small amount of rajma adds texture. Dairy cream is replaced with blended cashews and a finish of butter or oil plus kasuri methi.
This is a full comfort main with rice or naan-style flatbread. Plan soaking the night before; the active cook is mostly gentle simmering.
ingredients: 3/4 cup Whole Black Urad Dal (sabut urad)
2 tbsp Oil or Butter (plus more to finish)
1/2 tsp Sugar or jaggery (optional, balances tomato)
Cooked rice or roti for serving
instructions: Soak urad and rajma together 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Pressure-cook with fresh water and a pinch of salt until very soft—urad should mash easily (usually 5–7 whistles, then natural release). Reserve cooking liquid.
Blend soaked cashews with 1/4 cup water into a smooth cream.
Heat oil or butter. Crackle cumin. Saute onion until deep golden. Add ginger-garlic; cook until raw smell goes.
Add tomato puree, turmeric, chilli, coriander, and salt. Cook until thick and oil separates at the edges.
Add cooked dal-rajma with enough cooking liquid for a loose gravy. Mash some dal against the pot. Simmer 20–30 minutes on low, stirring often so it does not stick.
Stir in cashew cream, garam masala, kasuri methi, optional sugar, and a small finish of butter. Simmer 5 more minutes until creamy. Garnish with coriander. Serve hot with rice or roti.
stepNotes: Undercooked urad never becomes makhni—when in doubt, cook longer with more water.
Mashing a ladle of dal into the pot is the restaurant trick for body without dairy cream.
Kasuri methi is essential for the signature aroma; add it near the end so it stays fragrant.
If the gravy splits after cashew cream, lower the heat and stir continuously—do not boil hard.
Day-two dal makhni is better; thin with hot water when reheating.

variationSections.title: Richer or lighter
items: Richer: extra cashew cream and a spoon of coconut milk at the end.
Lighter: skip butter finish; use 2 tbsp cashews only.
Smoky: a 2-second charcoal dhungar with a drop of oil (optional, careful).

variationSections.title: No-onion / no-garlic
items: Skip onion and garlic; use hing, more ginger, tomato, and kasuri methi.
Finish with a little extra cashew cream for body.
Still pair with rice or a plain roti.

variationSections.title: Serving ideas
items: Jeera rice, plain basmati, or laccha-style paratha.
Onion rings and lemon on the side.
A small glass of chaas to cut the richness.
